refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional obligation: supporting teleoperation with a specified communication quality metric (packet loss limit). It does not combine multiple requirements and avoids specifying how the communication quality is achieved, thus remaining solution-neutral and atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit during teleoperation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters to meet packet loss requirements
Incorporate real-time network monitoring and dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters to ensure packet loss stays below threshold
Use buffering and smoothing techniques at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication protocol and network monitoring modules
Dependency on specific network conditions affecting protocol performance
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical communication protocol design that can be implemented independently of specific network hardware or policies, supporting modularity and independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters; unexpected network degradation may cause packet loss beyond limits
Adaptive error correction may introduce latency impacting teleoperation responsiveness
Worksite safety policies may impose constraints on communication methods that limit protocol options
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including network condition monitoring as a separate FR to better modularize adaptation mechanisms and reduce coupling between communication protocol and network variability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core communication and networking functionalities, including protocol implementation and adaptive error correction mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional interface for sending and receiving data packets over the network, essential for implementing communication protocols.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs status and performance data related to communication quality and error correction metrics.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the communication protocol and error correction logically belong here; minimal diffusion as most related functions are internal.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not implement protocol logic; would cause high diffusion if protocol implemented here.
Teleoperation depends on communication but is more about control commands; protocol implementation here would diffuse across modules.
Safety Services may monitor communication but is not responsible for protocol implementation; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the communication protocol with adaptive error correction, as it centralizes networking functions and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability and performance.
Risk of protocol changes impacting other modules if interfaces are not well defined.
Adaptive error correction complexity might require close coordination with Teleoperation for timing constraints.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limit, satisfying the acceptance criteria of packet loss ≤ 1%.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability and performance.
Adaptive error correction complexity might require close coordination with Teleoperation for timing constraints.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well contained within a single IM boundary. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation module to minimize diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics output via the Telemetry interface and validating that packet loss remains within the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation is clear as the NGHE Solution module encompasses the core communication and networking functionalities required to realize the FR, with well-defined external interfaces for network communication and telemetry.

recommended_revisions: Define clear interface contracts and performance requirements for the Network Link to mitigate dependency risks.
Establish coordination protocols between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to handle timing constraints related to adaptive error correction.
Document monitoring and verification procedures for packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface to ensure compliance with acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear verification feasibility, containment, and allocation clarity. The DP directly addresses the FR with an adaptive error correction protocol meeting the acceptance criteria. The IM (NGHE Solution module) shows high containment and low diffusion, minimizing architectural risks. Recommended revisions focus on interface contracts and coordination protocols but do not require further decomposition of the FR. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear, well-contained implementation module is preferable to further decomposition at this stage.

revise.why: To define clear interface contracts and coordination protocols to mitigate dependency and timing risks, and to document monitoring and verification procedures for packet loss metrics.

assumptions: The current DP fully satisfies the FR acceptance criteria under expected network conditions.
The NGHE Solution module can be effectively verified via telemetry monitoring.
Dependencies on Network Link and Teleoperation modules can be managed through interface contracts and coordination protocols.
open_risks: Dependency on Network Link interface stability and performance.
Complexity of adaptive error correction requiring coordination with Teleoperation for timing constraints.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing packet loss remains ≤1% under operational conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts and coordination protocols.
